title: Viterbi state probabilities as meta-learner feature (HMM, PIT-safe)
source: Conversation 2026-05-27: user asked whether Hidden Markov Models are compatible with the project. Lower-risk of the two HMM ideas surfaced.
why: An HMM fitted on returns/vol yields per-bar posterior state probabilities (soft regime membership). Exposed via the proposal contract's feature_overrides.add, these give the meta-learner a smooth, probabilistic view of regime that complements the existing hard 4-class label from pipeline/regimes.py. This does NOT touch the regime gate or the primary contract -- it is purely an additive meta feature, the slot the architecture already supports cleanly.
value_rationale: M, not H: it is additive information the meta may or may not find useful (MDA will tell). Upside is a richer regime signal; it cannot break existing invariants because it only enters as a candidate feature.
scope: Implement a PIT-safe HMM state-probability featurizer: re-estimate (Baum-Welch) on expanding-window training data only, never the full series; emit posterior P(state | data<=t) per bar.
Fix init + random_seed=42 and add a determinism test (same git_sha + data => identical feature column), per SKILL.md reproducibility requirement.
Anonymize state labels (state_0/state_1...) and confirm no calendar/date leakage into any dossier-facing artifact.
Wire as an entry in feature_overrides.add for a candidate proposal; verify lookahead_lint and the primary_input_contract test still pass.
Report MDA rank of the Viterbi feature(s) on at least one regime-conditional audit.
out_of_scope: Replacing pipeline/regimes.py as the regime labeler (that is B0060).

history.reason: Captured from HMM-compatibility discussion; low-risk additive-feature path.
